notableMoments: Achieved their best ever World Cup finish with third place in 1954 in Switzerland, beating Uruguay 3-1 in the third-place match
The legendary 'Wunderteam' of the 1930s was one of the best sides in the world, reaching the 1934 World Cup semifinal
Hans Krankl scored one of the greatest World Cup goals in 1978, a brilliant solo effort against Germany in a 3-2 Austrian victory in Córdoba
Coach Ralf Rangnick transformed the team with his high-pressing, gegenpressing style, turning Austria into one of Europe's most feared sides from 2024 onwards
Austria's involvement in the infamous 'Disgrace of Gijón' in 1982, the pre-arranged result with West Germany that eliminated Algeria
Returned to the World Cup in 2026 for the first time since 1998, ending a 28-year absence from the tournament
David Alaba became Austria's most decorated player ever, winning multiple Champions League and league titles with Bayern Munich and Real Madrid
The 2024 European Championship campaign under Rangnick, where Austria topped a group containing France, signaled the team's renaissance
